purpose: Domain registry for the machine-verifiable Research OS. Each domain plugs into the SAME pipeline (corpus -> frontier -> hypotheses -> tasks -> proofs -> truth graph -> site -> papers) by filling three replaceable slots: a claim corpus, an ontology/generator, and a verifier. secp256k1/ECDLP is instance #1 (live); the others are honest placeholders that reserve space without claiming results.
rule: A 'live' domain MUST have every slot file present and may reference live metric sources. A 'planned'/'exploratory' domain MUST NOT claim any metrics; its counts are null until it becomes live. Honesty is machine-checked by scripts/check_domains.py.

status_legend.live: real, kernel-verified content flowing through the whole pipeline
status_legend.planned: the same machinery clearly applies; corpus/proofs not started yet
status_legend.exploratory: plausible future direction that needs a different verifier or missing foundations

domains.title: ECDLP / secp256k1
domains.one_liner: Discrete-log hardness boundary for the Bitcoin/Ethereum curve, kernel-verified.
domains.notes: The reference instance. Everything the pipeline does, it does here first.

domains.title: NIST P-256 / secp256r1
domains.one_liner: The other dominant prime-field curve (TLS, passports). Same EC machinery, no CM/GLV.
domains.notes: LIVE (second real instance). Kernel-verified: both primes p and n (Pratt certificates, same generator as secp256k1), the unconditional EllipticCurve instance, the base point, and the j≠0/no-GLV structural contrast. Also kernel-verified since: generator order = n (weak keystone), embedding degree (MOV-resistance), non-anomalous trace (SSSA-resistance), and the generic-group Ω(√n) bound (curve-agnostic — the same `generic_dlog_query_bound`, stated for any prime, applies to P-256's n with no new proof). All nine starter-corpus rungs are now verified (domains/p256/corpus.md); the remaining gap is P-256's own strong keystone #E=n — secp256k1's was closed by a curve-specific certificate (CurveCardinalityExact.lean) that exploits j=0 structure P-256 lacks, so P-256 still needs Hasse or a new certificate route. Proves the pipeline is curve-agnostic — Phase-1 exit criterion met on verified facts.

domains.title: Elementary number theory (non-ECC portability instance)
domains.one_liner: First NON-elliptic-curve domain: primality / Mersenne / Carmichael facts, same kernel verifier — proof the engine is not ECC-only.
domains.notes: LIVE (first non-ECC instance). Kernel-verified in the separate `ResearchOS` lake library (a second build target alongside `Ecdlp`): 2017 prime, the Mersenne prime M₁₃=8191, and 561 (smallest Carmichael number) composite with its Korselt factorization 3·11·17. All `norm_num`, so they stay inside the standard axiom base (no `native_decide`); audited by `Ecdlp/AxiomAudit.lean`. Deliberately minimal — a proof of pipeline portability to an unrelated subject, not a number-theory research program. metrics_source is null (this domain does not feed the ECDLP headline count, which stays frozen).

domains.title: Curve25519 / Ed25519
domains.one_liner: The widely deployed twisted-Edwards curve (SSH, Signal, TLS 1.3).
domains.notes: Twisted Edwards form needs its own ontology, but the corpus/frontier/proof/site pipeline is unchanged. Reserved.

domains.title: Lattice / LWE post-quantum hardness
domains.one_liner: The post-quantum successor problem — a stress-test that the OS is not ECC-only.
domains.notes: Deliberately different: needs lattice-reduction / worst-case-to-average-case foundations largely absent from Mathlib, so the verifier slot is open. Listed to reserve the direction honestly, not to imply progress.

domains.title: Birch–Swinnerton-Dyer (elliptic-curve ranks / L-functions)
domains.one_liner: A Millennium-Prize conjecture on the SAME objects (elliptic curves) — a problem of a different order, not a parameter swap.
domains.notes: Shows the OS targets deep research, not only curve variants. Almost entirely blocked by missing Mathlib foundations (L-functions of elliptic curves, Tate–Shafarevich group, modularity), so its real near-term contribution would be an honest barrier map, not proofs. Reserved as a first-class exploratory direction.
